value.business_logic: The product is credible only if the viewer can connect the real brief, allowed memory, generated editable output, and review boundary in one glance.
value.why_this_proves_the_claim: The viewer sees the business input and editable output in one path, so the claim is not just a style promise.

value.headline: A real brief becomes editable PPT proof.
value.supporting_line: Commercial brief, curated source memory, and code-generated editable PPT appear as one product path.

value.business_logic: A deck can only explain its design decisions if source material is compiled into product memory and obligations before drawing starts.
value.why_this_proves_the_claim: The slide shows the mechanism that turns source inputs into obligations, so design choices have upstream reasons.

value.headline: Sources become product memory before drawing.
value.supporting_line: Allowed source observations become capability memory, message obligations, and proof objects.

value.speaker_note: Explain that tutorials and videos guide decisions only after they are converted into original product memory.

value.business_logic: The product difference is credible when the alternate path fails at a named missing mechanism and the Vulca path continues to editable output.
value.why_this_proves_the_claim: The contrast is a system boundary, not a beauty contest.

value.headline: The difference is mechanism, not taste.
value.supporting_line: Vulca binds source memory, proof routing, design moves, and editable output before the deck is claimed.

value.speaker_note: Name prompt-only and image-only paths as missing mechanism, not as inferior style by default.

value.business_logic: The product becomes memorable when the user sees the operating loop converge into one editable artifact rather than disconnected process cards.
value.why_this_proves_the_claim: The product moment is a generated editable result with proof attached, not a set of disconnected diagrams.

value.headline: One editable result owns the product moment.
value.supporting_line: Design memory, mapped design moves, code generation, and proof converge into one editable PPT preview.

value.speaker_note: Keep this slide cinematic and least report-like; proof should orbit the result, not compete with it.

value.business_logic: Credibility is preserved when the deck names the next proof and review gate instead of turning content readiness into a release claim.
value.why_this_proves_the_claim: The close is credible because it names the remaining proof instead of claiming release.

value.headline: The next proof is generated, not claimed.
value.supporting_line: This story becomes input for the next generated deck, then waits for review before release.

value.speaker_note: Name the remaining gate calmly: generated proof, visual review, native inspection, source-boundary review, and human approval.
